description: Single source of truth for stacks, versions, and module identifiers exposed by the interactive installer (`scripts/install.sh`) and the `/dhpk:setup` slash command. Adding a new module means: (1) add an entry here, (2) create modules/<id>/module.yaml + skills/, (3) register the skills path in .claude-plugin/plugin.json. Optional stack field `selection` defaults to `exclusive` (single-version pick); set to `additive` for stacks where library projects need to enable multiple versions cumulatively (e.g. a package spanning PHP 7.4 → 8.x). Optional per-version field `exclusive: true` (only meaningful under `additive`) marks a version that cannot combine with siblings in the same stack — the installer drops the others when an exclusive pick is selected.

stacks.name: PHP
stacks.description: PHP language baseline. Pick 5.6 for legacy monoliths (forbids 7.0+ syntax, polyfill guidance) — note 5.6 is marked exclusive and cannot combine with later versions. Pick 7.4 for the modern baseline (ships post-edit php-cs-fixer hook + pre-commit lint+phpstan+psalm gate). Pick 8.x additively on top of 7.4 for projects whose floor is ≥8.0, or for library packages that span 7.x→8.x and want guidance on conditionally-usable 8.x features (match, nullsafe, named args, attributes, readonly, enums, intersection types). Selection is additive — pick multiple versions to stack cumulative guidance.

stacks.name: Laravel Framework
stacks.description: Laravel framework — per-version modules for each major from 6 through 11. Library packages spanning multiple Laravel majors should enable all the versions in their composer constraint to get cumulative version-specific guidance (deprecations, signature additions, BC traps). Applications running a single Laravel major should pick only that version. Pair with skills/laravel-package-author/SKILL.md (cross-cutting, framework-agnostic).

stacks.name: Yii Framework
stacks.description: Yii PHP framework. The 1.1 module covers autoload, ActiveRecord / CDbCriteria, DDD layering, XSS/CSRF defaults.

stacks.name: PHPUnit
stacks.description: PHPUnit testing framework. Pick 5.7 for legacy PHP 5.6 / pre-PHPUnit-8 stacks. Pick 9 for PHP 7.3+ projects (latest version compatible with that PHP floor). Pick 10 for PHP 8.1+ projects (attribute-style annotations preferred). Pick 11 for PHP 8.2+ projects (doc-comment annotations fully removed; attributes-only). Selection is additive — a project mid-migration can enable two adjacent versions to consult both sets of guidance. The phpunit-10 / phpunit-11 modules document attribute migration paths; project's composer.json PHP constraint must independently satisfy each module's runtime requirement.

stacks.name: JavaScript / TypeScript
stacks.description: ESLint 9 flat config tier strategy, per-leaf `// @ts-check` progressive typing, post-edit ESLint feedback (async), pre-commit lint + typecheck gate. Framework-agnostic; works for legacy monolith bundles and modern bundler/SPA projects alike.

hook_profiles.description: Default profile. Sentinel-driven review reminders fire on relevant edits.
Standard + extra warnings (missing tools at SessionStart, docker container mismatch, etc.).

docker.notes: Container names are passed positionally to `--config docker_containers=<csv>`. The first entry is exported as DHPK_PHP_CONTAINER, the second as DHPK_MYSQL_CONTAINER, etc. Empty list disables the SessionStart docker check entirely.
